title: Farnsfield Halifax MZ519 Memorial
cause_of_loss: Anti-aircraft damage over Dieppe; engine fire

photos.caption: Wider view of the memorial site
Halifax III MZ519 LK-U of 578 Squadron
The Farnsfield memorial to the crew of MZ519
Close view of the memorial cross

narrative: The only operational combat loss in the Farnsfield area. P/O Parfitt's crew had bombed V-1 launch sites that afternoon. Crossing back over Dieppe, heavy anti-aircraft fire struck the Halifax. One engine caught fire. Parfitt attempted to nurse the stricken aircraft back to base at Burn, Yorkshire, but the fire spread. Witnesses at Farnsfield saw the aircraft pass with its wing ablaze. Local belief — supported by the crash trajectory — is that the crew deliberately chose not to bail out in order to steer the aircraft away from the village. All seven were killed on impact at Riddings Farm. The youngest, Canadian bomb aimer John Godin, was 19. This was Parfitt's 23rd operational mission.

crew.name: Reginald Parfitt
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm, Farnsfield on his 23rd operational mission. Local witnesses saw the aircraft pass with its wing ablaze. The crew are believed to have deliberately steered the aircraft away from the village.
crew.bio: A 22-year-old pilot from Cardiff on his 23rd operational mission. Local belief, supported by the crash trajectory, is that Parfitt and his crew deliberately chose not to bail out in order to steer the burning aircraft away from Farnsfield village.

crew.name: Raymond Albert Rolph
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm.

crew.name: Brian Grahame Turnidge
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm.

crew.name: John Joseph Godin
crew.fate_detail: The youngest crew member at just 19, Godin was a Canadian bomb aimer. Two Canadian redwoods were planted at the memorial site in his honour.
crew.bio: A 19-year-old Canadian from the RCAF, Godin was the youngest member of the crew. Two Canadian redwoods were planted at the Farnsfield memorial in his honour.
crew.burial_location: Harrogate Cemetery (Stonefall), Sec B, Row H, Grave 11

crew.name: Thomas Frederick Pitts
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm.

crew.name: Thomas Arthur Hill
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm.

crew.name: Leonard Granville Leatham
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Halifax MZ519 crashed at Riddings Farm.

memorial_details: Farnsfield residents Jack Wright and Frank Reynolds campaigned in the early 1990s to establish a permanent memorial. A stone memorial was dedicated on 6 July 1994 (50th anniversary) by Air Marshal Sir John Curtis, himself a former 578 Squadron pilot. Seven Nottinghamshire oaks (one per crew member) and two Canadian redwoods (for Godin) were planted. Parfitt Road on the village outskirts bears a second memorial plaque. The crew's names are read at every Remembrance Sunday service. The Farnsfield Memorial Trust maintains the site. A Nottinghamshire County Council walking trail includes the memorial.

maintainer.notes: Memorial maintained by Farnsfield Memorial Trust in cooperation with the parish council. The Trust maintains the stone memorial and surrounding oak trees.

local_contacts.org_name: Farnsfield & District Branch, Royal British Legion
local_contacts.role: Annual Remembrance Sunday service at the memorial
Maintains memorial site with Farnsfield Memorial Trust
Local history research and memorial documentation

external_narratives.summary: Detailed crew biographies and operational history for P/O Parfitt and crew. Includes CWGC records, squadron postings, and accounts from the 578 Squadron Association.
external_narratives.key_details: Confirms this was Parfitt's 23rd operational mission. Crew had completed training at 1660 HCU Swinderby before posting to 578 Squadron at Burn.
external_narratives.full_narrative: At 19:11 hours on 6 July 1944, Halifax III MZ519 (LK-U) of 578 Squadron took off from RAF Burn, Yorkshire on Operation Croixdalle — a daylight bombing raid on V-1 flying bomb launching sites at Croixdalle, France. At 22:25 hours, returning from the successful mission, the aircraft developed engine trouble. The pilot, P/O Reginald Parfitt (age 22, from Cardiff, on his 23rd operational mission), managed to avoid Farnsfield village but crashed at Riddings Farm. All seven crew members were killed. The recovered bodies were laid out in the small paddock in front of the farmhouse before their removal the following day — this left a lasting memory on the farmer and his family. As a memorial, seven Nottinghamshire oak trees were planted in a crescent to mark the spot, followed later by two Canadian redwood trees in memory of the one Canadian crew member, Fl/Sgt John Joseph Godin (age 19, RCAF). In the early 1990s, two locals — Frank Reynolds Jr and Jack Wright — had the idea of a more permanent memorial to mark the 50th anniversary of the crash. A committee was formed, funds raised, and the memorial was unveiled in 1994 by Air Marshal Sir John Curtis, who had himself flown in 578 Squadron. The area behind the original trees has been developed as a small copse through donations from villagers and others, maintained by the Farnsfield Memorial Trust. At the annual Remembrance Day Service, in addition to a roll call of villagers who died in the Two World Wars, a special mention is made of the names of the Halifax crew. There is also a memorial in the church which includes photographs of the seven crew members.

external_narratives.summary: Squadron records of the loss of Halifax MZ519 LK-U on 6 July 1944. Includes operational details of the V-1 site attack and eyewitness accounts from Farnsfield residents.
external_narratives.key_details: Local witnesses described the aircraft passing over with its port wing fully ablaze. The crew's deliberate decision to avoid the village is corroborated by the crash trajectory.

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museum register entry for the Halifax MZ519 memorial at Farnsfield. Records the stone memorial dedicated on 6 July 1994, the 50th anniversary of the crash, by Air Marshal Sir John Curtis.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial reference 32217. Dedicated by Air Marshal Sir John Curtis, himself a former 578 Squadron pilot. Seven Nottinghamshire oaks and two Canadian redwoods planted.

external_narratives.summary: War Memorials Online listing for the Halifax MZ519 memorial at Farnsfield, Nottinghamshire. Documents the memorial's condition as good and provides details of the crew commemorated.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial reference 92115. Condition recorded as good. Lists all seven crew members.

external_narratives.summary: Detailed crash investigation record for Halifax MZ519 LK-U. Documents the anti-aircraft damage sustained over Dieppe and the crew's attempt to return to RAF Burn with a spreading engine fire.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft attacked V-1 launch sites at Croixdalle. Heavily damaged by flak over Dieppe. Local witnesses saw the aircraft pass with its wing ablaze. Crash site at Riddings Farm.

location_confidence.verification_notes: CONFIRMED ERROR in v1. Previous coords (53.0836, -1.0234) placed pin at Farnsfield village centre.
